hero.name: Kyle Bush
hero.tagline: A classically trained software engineer designing resilient, well-architected cloud systems from the Pacific Northwest.
hero.sticky: Senior Solutions Architect at AWS, classically trained engineer. I design cloud systems that hold up under real load — from the PNW fog.

about.heading: I turn ambiguous problems into systems that hold up under load.

body.text: Senior Solutions Architect at AWS and a classically trained software engineer. I help teams design cloud systems that are secure, cost-aware, and boringly reliable — and I still read the source.
That classical foundation — data structures, distributed systems, the discipline of maintainable code — is the lens I bring to every architecture conversation.

projects.name: Cedar
projects.desc: A policy engine that lints CloudFormation & CDK against the Well-Architected pillars before anything deploys. Cedar turns tribal architecture knowledge into automated guardrails so teams catch cost, security, and reliability foot-guns in CI instead of in production.

projects.name: Fogline
projects.desc: A serverless status-page generator with edge caching and zero cold-start on the read path. Built to stay up when everything else is down — the read path never touches a cold Lambda.

projects.name: driftwood
projects.desc: A tiny CRDT library for offline-first apps, compiled to WASM and under 12kb gzipped. Conflict-free sync without dragging in a megabyte of dependencies.

projects.name: Trailhead CLI
projects.desc: A developer CLI that scaffolds a production-ready AWS account baseline — org structure, guardrails, logging, and identity — in one command.

projects.name: KyleOS
projects.desc: The desktop you are using right now. A portfolio reimagined as a tiny operating system, with draggable windows, a live dock, and a working menu bar.

publications.title: Designing for the failure you did not plan for
The cost of the abstraction you never questioned
A field guide to Well-Architected reviews
